Your accessible everyday assistant
Hello
How can I help you today?
Page 1 of 2 — Core tools
Voice Notes

Press Start, or Spacebar, to begin.

0 words
Text, not audio: Voice Notes transcribes your speech into words — Save produces a .txt file (readable text, not a playable recording), and Send to WhatsApp sends this text to your primary emergency contact. For an actual audio recording you can send as a voice file, use Messages → Voice Messages instead.
PathSense
Press "Start Camera" to begin scanning.

Press Start Camera, or Spacebar, to begin.

    Needs internet. The detection model downloads from an external AI service — if your network blocks that, the camera preview still works but detection won't.
    Read Aloud

    Type or paste text, then press Read.

    Works fully offline. This uses your device's built-in voice, not an internet-based one — no network needed.
    SOS

    Hold the button for 2 seconds to alert your primary contact.

    How it works: Captures your GPS location, then opens your phone's own SMS/dialer app pre-filled with the alert. The hold-button alerts your first (primary) contact — use the Call/Message buttons above for others. Manage contacts in Settings.
    Scan Text
    No image selected yet.

    Choose a photo of printed text to begin.

    Needs internet. Text extraction downloads an OCR engine from an external service the first time you use it.
    Navigation
    Speak a Destination

    Tap, then say a place — opens directions in Google Maps.

    Saved Places
    Search Nearby
    How this works: Every option here opens Google Maps directly, already pointed at your destination and centered on your current location — nothing to type. Saved Places and Search Nearby go straight to a ready-to-start walking route (tap Start once Maps opens); Speak Destination lets you say anywhere else out loud. Google Maps then handles live, turn-by-turn voice-guided walking directions — this is more reliable than anything built directly into Lumen.
    Currency
    Start the live camera, or import a photo below.
    No photo imported yet.

    Photograph the note, flat and well-lit.

    —
    Result appears here
    How this works: reads the printed denomination number off the note using text recognition (OCR) — it's not a trained currency-classifier model, so it needs the number to be clearly visible and in focus. Live camera re-scans every few seconds automatically. Needs internet the first time (downloads the OCR engine).
    Faces
    Press "Start Camera" to begin.

    Start the camera, then enroll a face by name.

    No one enrolled yet.
    How this works: uses face-api.js, a real face-recognition library, running entirely in your browser. Enrolled faces only last for this session (not saved after closing the page). Needs internet to download the recognition models the first time.
    Explore
    About these tools: Learn Braille and Daily Tips work fully offline. Audio Library's Books/Stories/Quotes are read by your device's voice (no internet needed) — "My Files" plays real audio you import.

    Discover tools and resources

    Learn Braille
    Audio Library
    Daily Tips
    —
    Messages
    Emergency Contacts
    Volunteers
    Voice Messages
    What this is for: quick spoken reminders you can record hands-free, then press WhatsApp to open your phone's share sheet and send the audio to any contact, or Clear to discard it. Note: the app automatically picks the most WhatsApp-compatible recording format your browser supports — but on some Android/Chrome combinations, only a format WhatsApp may reject is available. If sharing fails, use the Download fallback and attach the file manually.

    Press Record to start a voice message.

    Emergency Contacts

    The first contact in this list is your "primary" — the SOS hold-button alerts them automatically.
